content.problemStatement:
Old Farmer Ranjan works on the farm of landlord Mittal Sahab. On the occasion of Diwali, Mittal Sahab decided to give some bonus to the hardworking farmer Ranjan. Mittal Sahab promised him to offer all the dragon fruits that Ranjan collects in one go from the right side of the farm returning back to the same side, such that he can take exactly 2 left turns.
Ranjan farmer can start plowing from any cell A[i][N]A[i][N]A[i][N] where 1 <=i ii <= N.
The farm is represented as a 2D matrix, each cell consisting of some units of dragon fruit. Ranjan farmer wants to collect as much amount of dragon fruits as possible, but he is illiterate so he gave this job to his son, the great coder Rishi.
Please refer to the image below for more clarification on how to traverse the farm.


###Input


The first line contains single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The next line consists of 2 space separated integers NNN and MMM, denoting the dimension of the farm.
The next NNN lines consist of MMM space separated integers each denoting the amount of dragon fruit in the cell.
###Output:
Output a single integer denoting the maximum amount of vegetable Ranjan farmer could collect.

###Constraints


1≤T≤51 \leq T \leq 51≤T≤5
2≤N,M≤1032 \leq N,M \leq 10^32≤N,M≤103
2≤A[i][j]≤1092 \leq A[i][j] \leq 10^92≤A[i][j]≤109 for  1≤i,j≤N,Mrespectively1 \leq i,j \leq N,M respectively1≤i,j≤N,Mrespectively
###Sample Input:
2
3 3
1 2 3
3 3 1
4 1 6
3 3
3 7 4
1 9 6
1 7 7

###Sample Output:
20
34
constraints: 2 ≤ A[i][j] ≤ 10^9 for 1 ≤ i, j ≤ N, M respectively
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, denoting the number of test cases. The next line consists of 2 space-separated integers N and M, denoting the dimension of the farm. The next N lines consist of M space-separated integers each denoting the amount of dragon fruit in the cell.
content.outputFormat: Output a single integer denoting the maximum amount of dragon fruit Ranjan farmer could collect.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum amount of dragon fruit that can be collected is 20. For the second test case, the maximum amount of dragon fruit that can be collected is 34.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to find the maximum amount of dragon fruit that can be collected. We will create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the maximum amount of dragon fruit that can be collected starting from cell (i, j) and making exactly two left turns. We will fill up the dp array by iterating over all cells and considering all possible moves.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * M) — where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of rows, and M is the number of columns.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * M) — for the dp array.

code.Python3:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N, M = map(int, input().split())
    grid = [list(map(int, input().split())) for _ in range(N)]
    maxCollect = 0
    for i in range(N):
        collect = 0
        x, y = i, M - 1
        collect += grid[x][y]
        # first left turn
        firstTurn = -1
        for j in range(M - 2, -1, -1):
            if grid[x][j] > firstTurn:
                firstTurn = grid[x][j]
                y = j
        collect += firstTurn
        # second left turn
        secondTurn = -1
        for j in range(x - 1, -1, -1):
            if grid[j][y] > secondTurn:
                secondTurn = grid[j][y]
        collect += secondTurn
        maxCollect = max(maxCollect, collect)
    print(maxCollect)
